mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate near zero compresses the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, which increases present valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent indicates persistent labor market slack that weakens household income growth and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based demand recovery that reduces recession probability and lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of coincident months, or interaction term Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during early-cycle recovery phases reflects rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which drives positive earnings revisions and short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum in post-recession periods fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 52% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation combined with a federal funds rate trapped near zero erodes real corporate profit margins and signals future policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 2.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: The joint inflation and low rate condition produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term Rank IC exceeds zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly captures resilient household consumption, which supports top-line revenues for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents and produces positive returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Contemporaneous RSAFS growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day returns with Rank IC below 0.02, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of expanding sales months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates income-supported demand rather than credit-driven spending, which sustains corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS and RSAFS growth does not generate positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months, or the interaction-driven Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 600 thousand units with positive two-month momentum trigger construction sector multiplier effects and household wealth gains that lift broad S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 600 and 2-month momentum is positive
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST levels produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
